entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A cereal crop cultivated in India, subject to lodging.

entities.name: LODGING TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a genotype to resist falling over due to wind and rain.

entities.name: CULM STRENGTH
entities.description: The strength of the plant stem, used as a proxy trait for selecting lodging tolerant genotypes.

entities.name: PLANT HEIGHT PER STEM WEIGHT
entities.description: A morpho-physiological trait that exhibited significant differences between lodging tolerant and susceptible hybrids.

entities.name: CROWN ROOT ANGLE
entities.description: The angle of the root spread, which is positively correlated with culm strength.

entities.name: DIGITAL PLANT STEM STRENGTH METER
entities.description: A tool (Model YVD-18) used to measure the strength of the plant culm.

entities.name: RAINY SEASON
entities.description: A cultivation period characterized by rains and high velocity winds.

entities.name: SUMMER SEASON
entities.description: A cultivation period often under irrigated environments.

entities.name: HIGH VELOCITY WINDS
entities.description: Environmental factor that contributes to crop lodging.

entities.name: RAINS
entities.description: Environmental factor that contributes to crop lodging.

entities.name: WELL-WATERED ENVIRONMENT
entities.description: An environment identified as optimal for evaluating lodging tolerance in pearl millet.

entities.name: WATER STRESS
entities.description: A condition of limited irrigation after grain set where culm strength differences were non-significant.

entities.name: MILKY GRAIN STAGE
entities.description: A pre-harvest growth stage used for measuring culm strength.

entities.name: CROP MATURITY STAGE
entities.description: The stage of crop maturity or harvesting used for measuring culm strength.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where pearl millet hybrids are cultivated.

entities.name: YIELD LOSSES
entities.description: The reduction in crop output resulting from lodging.
